name: checklist-full-v4
description: Reliance-grade audit checklist — 57 rules across 10 sections. Source-traceable to financial_model_audit_checklist.docx. Lean rule structure — methodology lives in skill.md.

tier1.label: No Excel formula errors anywhere in workbook
tier1.fix_instruction: Formula errors must be investigated and corrected at source by the modeller. Never replace with a hardcoded value or mask with IFERROR. Locate the root cause: broken link, missing reference, division by zero, or lookup failure.

tier1.label: Minimum sheet structure present
tier1.fix_instruction: File must contain at least 2 recognisable financial model sheets. Verify the correct file was uploaded.

tier1.label: Model Issues tab is empty
tier1.fix_instruction: Resolve all open items in the Model Issues tab before submitting for reliance review.

tier1.label: Balance sheet check sheet exists
tier1.fix_instruction: Add a balance sheet check row showing Assets minus Liabilities minus Equity equals zero for every period.

tier1.label: Cash flow reconciliation sheet exists
tier1.fix_instruction: Add a cash reconciliation check row: change in balance sheet cash equals operating plus investing plus financing cash flows.

tier1.label: No negative PP&E values
tier1.fix_instruction: Gross PP&E cannot be negative. Review the asset roll-forward on the AFS sheet.

tier2.label: Model purpose and audience documented
tier2.fix_instruction: Add a cover sheet or README documenting model purpose, audience, reliance level, and key limitations.

tier2.label: FAST principles applied — no opaque constructs
tier2.fix_instruction: Simplify flagged formula. Break into intermediate steps. Remove opaque constructs.

tier2.label: Inputs centralised — no business assumptions hard-coded in calculation sheets
tier2.fix_instruction: Move hard-coded business assumptions to the Inputs sheet and replace with cell references.

tier2.label: No duplicated calculation logic across sheets
tier2.fix_instruction: Remove duplicate calculation blocks. Each calculation should exist once and be referenced elsewhere.

tier2.label: No mixed monthly and quarterly logic on same sheet
tier2.fix_instruction: Annual views must be explicit aggregations only. No independent annual logic that can diverge from the monthly or quarterly engine.

tier2.label: No critical calculations on output sheets
tier2.fix_instruction: Move calculation logic to a supporting schedule sheet and replace with a reference link on the output sheet.

tier2.label: Single master timeline drives all time-series sheets
tier2.fix_instruction: Link all sheet timelines to the master timeline on the Inputs or Timing sheet.

tier2.label: No hidden sheets affecting results
tier2.fix_instruction: Unhide all sheets and confirm none contain material calculations affecting outputs without disclosure.

tier2.label: Model extendable without formula rewrites
tier2.fix_instruction: Confirm that adding extra periods, assets, or debt facilities can be done by copying existing blocks without rewriting logic.

tier2.label: No volatile functions in core model engine
tier2.fix_instruction: Replace OFFSET and INDIRECT functions in core calculation areas with static references or structured table logic.

tier2.label: All inputs on dedicated sheets with labels, units, and source
tier2.fix_instruction: Consolidate all inputs to the Inputs sheet. Add labels, units, sign conventions, source references, and last update date.

tier2.label: No business assumptions hard-coded in formula areas
tier2.fix_instruction: Move all business assumptions (price, volume, margin, tax rate, debt terms, capex) to the Inputs sheet and replace formula constants with cell references.

tier2.label: Historical data reconciles to source documents
tier2.fix_instruction: Add reconciliation tables showing historical financial statements tie to source documents (audited FS, management accounts).

tier2.label: Historical data is current and not stale
tier2.fix_instruction: Update historical data to the most recent available period. Flag any data sets that are outdated relative to the model start date.

tier2.label: Key assumptions entered once and referenced consistently
tier2.fix_instruction: Centralise all key assumptions on the Inputs sheet and replace all instances elsewhere with a single cell reference.

tier2.label: Single formula per row on all time-series
tier2.fix_instruction: Standardise the formula across each flagged row. Check for mid-row edits, broken copy-paste, or anchoring inconsistencies.

tier2.label: Consistent sign convention throughout workbook
tier2.fix_instruction: Review and standardise sign conventions. Document the chosen convention on the Inputs sheet.

tier2.label: No unintended circular references
tier2.fix_instruction: Document any intentional circular references on the Inputs sheet. Confirm they converge reliably and can be switched off for audit.

tier2.label: Unit consistency and conversion checks
tier2.fix_instruction: Check for unit conversion errors (monthly vs annual rates, MW vs kW, etc.). Add explicit unit labels to all input rows.

tier2.label: Lookup function integrity
tier2.fix_instruction: Review all VLOOKUP, HLOOKUP, INDEX/MATCH, and XLOOKUP functions. Confirm range references are correct and match types are appropriate.

tier2.label: Balance sheet balances for every period
tier2.fix_instruction: Balance sheet does not balance — critical error. Review equity roll-forward, retained earnings, and debt closing balances on AFS, IFS, and Debt sheets.

tier2.label: Change in cash equals net cash flow for every period
tier2.fix_instruction: Cash flow does not reconcile to balance sheet. Review all three cash flow sections for missing items or double-counted flows.

tier2.label: Net income flows through to retained earnings
tier2.fix_instruction: Retained earnings roll-forward does not reconcile. Review the link from the IFS net profit line to the retained earnings opening balance.

tier2.label: Equity movements reconcile between balance sheet and cash flow
tier2.fix_instruction: Equity movement appears in balance sheet but not in financing cash flow or vice versa. Reconcile the equity schedule to the financing activities section.

tier2.label: Working capital movements reconcile between balance sheet and operating cash flow
tier2.fix_instruction: Working capital movement in balance sheet does not match operating cash flow. Review receivables, payables, and inventory links.

tier2.label: No logically impossible balance sheet positions
tier2.fix_instruction: Logically impossible balance sheet value found. Review the flagged line item and correct the formula or assumption.

tier2.label: Income tax calculation is reconcilable
tier2.fix_instruction: Tax calculation appears disconnected from taxable profits. Review the tax schedule and verify the effective rate reconciliation.

tier2.label: Deferred tax treatment disclosed
tier2.fix_instruction: Add a deferred tax schedule reconciling temporary differences, or document the simplification explicitly on the Inputs sheet.

tier2.label: Debt roll-forward identity holds for each facility
tier2.fix_instruction: Debt roll-forward does not balance. Review drawdown, repayment, capitalised interest, FX effects, and fee amortisation links on the Debt sheet.

tier2.label: Interest calculated from referenced rates — no hard-coded rates
tier2.fix_instruction: Replace any hard-coded interest rates in the Debt sheet with references to the Inputs sheet base rate and margin assumptions.

tier2.label: Covenant calculations follow documented definitions
tier2.fix_instruction: Review covenant calculation definitions against the term sheet. Update formulas to match the documented definitions exactly.

tier2.label: Capex phasing feeds into PP&E, balance sheet and investing cash flow
tier2.fix_instruction: Review the Capex schedule links to both the AFS balance sheet and the Cons investing activities section.

tier2.label: Cash waterfall sequence is valid
tier2.fix_instruction: Review the cash waterfall sequence: revenue → O&M → taxes → debt service → reserves → distributions. Confirm each step is correctly implemented.

tier2.label: Cash sweep and lock-up mechanisms operate correctly
tier2.fix_instruction: Review cash sweep and lock-up logic. Under covenant breach, distributions should be blocked and excess cash directed to debt pay-down or trapped in reserves per the term sheet.

tier2.label: DSRA and reserve accounts reconcile correctly
tier2.fix_instruction: Review DSRA and other reserve accounts. Confirm they correctly size, fund, release, and replenish, tying to both cash flow and balance sheet.

tier2.label: Revenue equals price times volume consistently
tier2.fix_instruction: Add explicit price times volume calculations on the Ops sheet referencing price inputs from the Inputs sheet.

tier2.label: Production volumes within installed capacity
tier2.fix_instruction: Review production volume assumptions. Volumes must not exceed modelled capacity in any period without a corresponding capacity expansion event.

tier2.label: Fixed versus variable cost classification is sensible
tier2.fix_instruction: Review cost classification. Variable costs should move with volume in a plausible way. Fixed costs should not spike without a documented driver.

tier2.label: Margins plausible relative to history and industry norms
tier2.fix_instruction: Review margin assumptions on the Inputs sheet. Compare against historical periods and industry benchmarks for the specific commodity and operation type.

tier2.label: IRR, NPV and valuation metrics within plausible range
tier2.fix_instruction: Review discount rate assumptions and cash flow profile. Compare return metrics against risk and market practice for the specific project type.

tier2.label: Scenario engine centrally controlled
tier2.fix_instruction: Centralise scenario selection to a single input cell on the Inputs or Dashboard sheet. Link all scenario-dependent assumptions through consistent INDEX or IF logic.

tier2.label: Core sensitivities produce directionally correct responses
tier2.fix_instruction: Review the link between the sensitivity input and the affected output. A counter-intuitive result usually indicates a mis-linked formula or reversed sign convention.

tier2.label: Zero revenue stress test passes
tier2.fix_instruction: Setting main revenue drivers to zero should produce zero revenue, logical shutdown of variable costs, and a structurally intact model with no error explosions.

tier2.label: Date and period edge cases do not break model
tier2.fix_instruction: Test partial periods and period boundary shifts. Misaligned formulas or broken logic in edge cases must be corrected.

tier2.label: Dedicated checks sheet aggregates all critical tests
tier2.fix_instruction: Add a dedicated Checks sheet aggregating all critical model integrity tests with clear PASS or FAIL flags, sheet references, and cell references for each test.

tier2.label: Change log exists with version history
tier2.fix_instruction: Add a change log tab or section on the cover sheet. Record date, editor, description of changes, and version tag for each material edit.

tier2.label: Independent review evidence recorded
tier2.fix_instruction: Add a note on the cover sheet or change log indicating that a peer or independent review has been conducted, with date and scope noted.

tier2.label: Dashboard presents core KPIs and links to calculations
tier2.fix_instruction: Replace any pasted values on the Dashboard with live cell references. Verify all charts and tables update correctly under scenario changes.

tier2.label: Cover sheet documents model purpose, structure and limitations
tier2.fix_instruction: Add or complete the cover sheet with model purpose, version, author, date, structure overview, scenario controls, key assumptions, known limitations, and instructions for running checks.

tier2.label: Output sheets readable on screen and in print
tier2.fix_instruction: Set consistent print areas, fonts, and column widths. Remove cut-off tables. Ensure dashboard is readable without horizontal scrolling.

tier2.label: Simplifications explicitly documented
tier2.fix_instruction: Document all known simplifications on the cover sheet or Inputs sheet (e.g. deferred tax excluded, leases simplified, minor revenue streams excluded).
